threats.title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
threats.description: The system may be vulnerable to data injection attacks if user input is not properly validated or sanitized before being used in database queries or other operations. An attacker could inject malicious SQL code or other harmful data to manipulate the system's data or functionality.
threats.attack_vector: User input directly used in database queries without proper sanitization or parameterized queries.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.

threats.title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
threats.description: If the system uses direct references to internal objects (e.g., database IDs, file names) without proper authorization checks, an attacker could potentially access resources or perform actions that they are not authorized to perform by manipulating these references.
threats.attack_vector: Direct manipulation of object identifiers in requests to access unauthorized resources.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks for all requests that access internal objects.
Use indirect references or token-based authentication instead of direct object references.
Regularly audit API endpoints for potential IDOR vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Weak Authentication Bypass
threats.description: An attacker could potentially bypass authentication mechanisms due to weak or missing authentication factors. This could involve exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication process itself, or leveraging default credentials or known vulnerabilities in the authentication libraries used.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication logic, using brute-force attacks against weak passwords, or leveraging default credentials.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Enforce strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Regularly audit authentication logs for suspicious activity and unusual login attempts.

threats.title: Lack of Audit Logging
threats.description: Insufficient audit logging makes it difficult to track user actions, identify security breaches, and determine accountability. Without proper logging, it becomes impossible to investigate incidents, reconstruct events, or demonstrate compliance with regulations.
threats.attack_vector: No comprehensive logging of user actions, system events, or data modifications.
mitigations: Implement comprehensive audit logging of all critical system events, including user logins, data access, and configuration changes.
Regularly review audit logs for suspicious activity and potential security breaches.
Establish a robust log retention policy to ensure sufficient data is available for forensic analysis.

threats.title: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Missing Access Controls
threats.description: Without defined access controls and data classification, sensitive data could be exposed to unauthorized users or systems. This could occur if data is stored without proper encryption or if users have excessive permissions.
threats.attack_vector: Unauthorized access to sensitive data due to insufficient access controls, weak encryption, or misconfigured permissions.
mitigations: Implement strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege.
Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit.
Regularly review and update access permissions to ensure they remain appropriate.

An attacker could inject malicious SQL code or other harmful data to manipulate the system's data or functionality.
mapped_threats.attack_vector: User input directly used in database queries without proper sanitization or parameterized queries.

gaps: Lack of comprehensive audit logging coverage
Insufficient access control implementation across multiple areas
priorities: Medium - Weak Authentication Bypass and Lack of Audit Logging

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Bypass
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Lack of Audit Logging
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-778
    owasp: A09:2021
    threat_category: Repudiation
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Missing Access Controls
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
